title: epipactis helleborine subsp. ohwii
intro: Epipactis helleborine subsp. ohwii, also known as the Japanese helleborine, is a rare and endangered subspecies of orchid native to Japan. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Epipactis helleborine subsp. ohwii are said to include relief from fever, sore throats, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to stimulate the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions and decoctions, which are typically used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
